paper_title: Blow-ups and extensions of trees in tournaments
summary: Conjecture 10 from arXiv:2410.23566 asserts the existence of an absolute constant C such that every k-extension of an oriented tree of order n is C^k(2n-2)-unavoidable. This would follow from Conjecture 9 (multiplicative stability under vertex addition) combined with Sumner's conjecture, but remains open. The paper itself establishes only a weaker bound of (2·3^{\binom{2k+2}{2}}·|V(F)|)-unavoidability (Corollary 25), with super-exponential dependence on k rather than the conjectured simple exponential C^k. No subsequent work resolving or further advancing this conjecture was found in the published literature.
search_queries: k-extension oriented tree unavoidable tournament Aboulker Havet Lochet Lopes 2410.23566
blow-up extension oriented tree tournament unavoidable Sumner conjecture 2025
Picasarri-Arrieta Rambaud Lopes tournament oriented tree 2025 arXiv unavoidable
notes: No follow-up resolving or partially resolving Conjecture 10 was found after 5 web calls. The conjecture is implied by the still-open Conjecture 9 of the same paper together with the (also open) exact form of Sumner's conjecture. Lucas Picasarri-Arrieta's personal page and Clément Rambaud's arXiv listing show no 2025 papers on this topic.
